metric_value: 6 of 8 firms have centralized QA organizations that actively conduct project reviews; remaining 2 have centralized standards bodies with reviews done by senior partners
notes: Summary finding across all 8 firms

metric_value: Only 3 of 8 firms (Deloitte CSC Keane) share complete QA reports with clients as general practice
notes: EDS and PwC share only summaries if requested

metric_value: Majority of firms do not use QA reviews to determine employee performance to encourage openness
notes: Critical for ensuring openness in QA process

metric_value: None of the 8 suppliers share pre-engagement risk assessments with potential clients
notes: Risk assessments used internally for profitability decisions

metric_value: None of the interviewees could recall a single instance where internal QA documents were used in legal actions
notes: Significant finding given IE v. Andersen litigation context

metric_value: Andersen's internal QA standards and practices are equal to or above industry norms in centralized coordination and mandated processes
notes: Key conclusion of the study

metric_value: Andersen requires all projects undergo at least one independent QA review; this is above industry norms where reviews are typically risk/revenue-based
notes: Based on review of Client Quality Management Reference Manual

metric_value: Quality Management System (QMS) covers all quality management from internal best practices to external client satisfaction; CQM component manages engagement quality
notes: Hierarchical structure: Managing Partner for Quality > Quality Leadership Council > Area/Region QM Partners > Practice Unit QM

metric_value: Every engagement requires Engagement Risk Assessment (ERA) at opportunity stage; engagement partner decides based on risk level whether independent CQMA is needed
notes: Policy-level requirement documented in CQM Reference Manual

metric_value: CQMAs may occur at opportunity assessment and proposal preparation and contractual commitment and execution stages; at least one during execution is mandatory
notes: Review schedule is part of every engagement Quality Plan

metric_value: Quality Advisor program started 1992; outgrowth of internal restructuring triggered by unhappy client engagements from Ernst and Whinney / Arthur Young merger
notes: Program managed by Director of Risk Management US

metric_value: Quality Advisor assigned to all engagements with revenues above $1M; advisor at partner level or higher and recognized expert in practice area
notes: Reports to practice manager not engagement team

metric_value: Quarterly reviews lasting up to 2-3 days; red/yellow/green flag system for findings; poor QA review can negatively affect engagement manager bonus
notes: Reviews cover engagement status and resource allocation and problems and customer satisfaction

metric_value: Provides summary but not detailed reports of quarterly review to clients based on advice of internal counsel
notes: Quality Advisor program described in pre-sales materials and proposals

metric_value: TMG at CMM Level 2 working toward Level 3; Federal unit at Level 4; two federal units (SEAS and Aerospace IT) at Level 5
notes: Only organization in professional services with two Level 5 units

metric_value: SEAS earned ISO 9001 certification in 1997
notes: Federal business unit achievement

metric_value: TMG overseeing QA for commercial business units since 1996; does not conduct QA testing but ensures processes modeled after SEI practices
notes: Oversees test plan development and performs scheduled process checks

metric_value: QA reports based on Balanced Scorecard approach tracking errors and corrections and progress on outstanding errors
notes: Release-based schedule with client involvement in acceptance testing

metric_value: Corporate Knowledge Program supported by Lotus Notes database capturing plans and processes and procedures available worldwide
notes: Includes QA-related documentation

metric_value: Interviewee could not recall a single legal action against CSC for software projects since 1996 when TMG was established
notes: Some prior instances of fixing problems for clients at no charge

metric_value: KPMG moved from post-engagement quality review to proactive QA model in 1996 coinciding with consulting reorganization
notes: Driven by larger more complex national engagements

metric_value: QAP under National Risk Management Partner staffed by 3 senior partners; any engagement over $1M assigned QAP team member
notes: Monthly team meetings to review open engagements and select for review

metric_value: 4-5 day reviews including client interviews (2-4 key personnel); red/yellow/green risk flags; monthly follow-up for red-flagged projects
notes: Reports sent to engagement team and practice leader and KPMG executive team

metric_value: KPMG in process of phased rollout of knowledge management tool to consolidate engagement histories and best practices with practice-specific templates
notes: In progress at time of study

metric_value: Deloitte separates Risk Management (corporate function with National Partner) and QA/QC (practice-level Senior Partner) into entirely separate functions
notes: Risk Management Partner can veto proposed engagements above $1M based on weighted score

metric_value: Deloitte rarely engages in projects where employees are wholly responsible for end product; project teams work with client staff and are jointly managed
notes: QA assessments done by practice members not external reviewers

metric_value: European and Canadian practices ISO 9000 certified; some Asia/Pacific (notably Australia); US practices not ISO 9000 but evaluating SEI CMMs and PMI certification
notes: US aligning with Project Management Institute

metric_value: US practices formalizing QA methodologies across all practice areas under Senior Partner; also creating consistent global Risk Management review templates
notes: Responsibility will probably remain within each practice

metric_value: Corporate QA organization established 1994 headed by Director of QA; prior to that QA handled at area VP level across 5 geographic areas
notes: Centralized during rapid growth period starting 1997

metric_value: UK operations ISO 9001 certified; certification required to do business in most European countries; also pursuing SEI CMM Level 3 on project-by-project basis
notes: Typically achieves Level 3 within 8-12 months

metric_value: Every deliverables-based project reviewed quarterly by QA organization; reviews last 1 day to 2 weeks; formal reports shared with both Keane management and client staff
notes: PCRP tracks financials and schedules and milestones and deliverables

metric_value: QA reports and review findings scrubbed of proprietary info and archived in internal knowledge management system for best practices
notes: Review results not tied to compensation

metric_value: QA responsibility rests with 75+ solutions centers and SBUs; not mandated from corporate level; CM&P organization since 1988 develops guidelines and templates
notes: Highly autonomous structure with diverse internal standards

metric_value: PM2 (ISO-influenced project management 2nd generation) and SC3 (SEI-based system lifecycle 3rd iteration since 1988); neither CMM-certified nor ISO-compliant
notes: Roughly half of 75+ solutions centers follow PM2; three using SC3

metric_value: More than 75 solutions centers plus strategic account groups and strategic business units
notes: Indicates scale and diversity of EDS operations

metric_value: While standards adherence not always mandated internally it can be driven by customer requirements; many units use ISO or SEI-certified processes independently
notes: Some units predate corporate methodologies

metric_value: Three major geographic regions each have QA and risk management organization; functions formalized in 1988 from Price Waterhouse heritage
notes: Americas; Europe/Middle East/Africa; Asia/Pacific

metric_value: Individual QA reports archived until subsequent reports issued then destroyed; final reports destroyed one year after engagement completion
notes: Reports can be scrubbed for best practices before destruction

metric_value: Clients do not receive copies of reports unless specifically requested; QA reviews seldom identify issues not already noted by engagement teams
notes: Reviews primarily ensure adherence to internal best practices

metric_value: E&Y and KPMG both use $1M revenue threshold for mandatory QA review assignment; Deloitte Risk Management also triggers above $1M
notes: Cross-firm comparison of review triggers
